articles.title: AI Coding Tools Tricked Into Hacking Developer Machine via Decades-Old Technique
articles.summary: Wiz has disclosed the details of a new AI coding assistant attack method it has dubbed GhostApproval. The post AI Coding Tools Tricked Into Hacking Developer Machine via Decades-Old Technique appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: AssuranceAmerica data breach exposes records of 6.9 million drivers
articles.summary: American insurance company AssuranceAmerica has disclosed a data breach impacting nearly 7 million drivers after attackers gained access to its systems earlier this year. [...]

articles.title: Chrome 150 Update Patches 27 Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: The security refresh resolves 13 use-after-free bugs, including two critical-severity flaws found by Google. The post Chrome 150 Update Patches 27 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Microsoft patches RoguePlanet Defender zero-day vulnerability
articles.summary: Microsoft has released a security patch to address a Defender zero-day vulnerability known as "RoguePlanet," disclosed after the June 2026 Patch Tuesday. [...]

articles.title: Top AI Agents Built to Catch Malicious Code Can Be Tricked Into Running It
articles.summary: Ask an AI coding agent to scan open-source code for security holes, and it might run the attacker's code on your own machine instead. articles.title: Unpatched Backdoor in Tenda Firmware Grants Admin Access to Devices
articles.summary: Tracked as CVE-2026-11405, the vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to access a device's web management interface. The post Unpatched Backdoor in Tenda Firmware Grants Admin Access to Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: GhostApproval Symlink Flaws Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code in AI Coding Agents
articles.summary: Researchers at Wiz found that a flaw in six popular AI coding assistants lets a booby-trapped code project quietly take control of a developer's computer. The assistant asks permission to edit one harmless-looking file, but the write lands on a sensitive one instead. The affected tools are

articles.title: Fake 7-Zip Installers Turn Devices Into Residential Proxy Nodes
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new threat actor dubbed Lurking Lizard that has been operating an end-to-end malicious residential proxy business using an infrastructure comprising more than 230 lookalike domains. The activity dates back to at least August 2022, according to DN

articles.title: Mount Royal University confirms breach as hackers claim attack
articles.summary: Mount Royal University in Calgary says hackers stole and then deleted data from its file storage systems after breaching the university's network. [...]

articles.title: Fake Paysafe, Skrill SDKs on NPM and PyPi steal credentials
articles.summary: Malicious packages on the Node Package Manager (npm) and the Python Package Index (PyPI) delivered stealer malware to developers and users of Paysafe, Skrill, and Neteller payment applications. [...]

articles.title: Hackers exploit Roundcube flaw to spy on academic researchers
articles.summary: A China-linked threat cluster has been exploiting vulnerable Roundcube servers at U.S. and Canadian universities to steal credentials and deploy backdoor malware. [...]

articles.title: AI Coding Agents Found Triggering Endpoint Security Rules Built to Catch Attackers
articles.summary: Sophos looked at a week of its own endpoint data and found that AI coding agents such as Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenAI Codex are setting off detection rules written to catch human intruders. The agents are not malicious. They just do a lot of things that, to a behavioral engine, look exactly like

articles.title: Taiwan charges two businessmen over alleged role in Chinese espionage campaign
articles.summary: A company based in Taiwan was leasing out accounts on the popular LINE messaging app to Chinese spies, according to prosecutors, who charged two men in the alleged scheme.

articles.title: Entra passkey enrollment vishing targets Microsoft 365 users
articles.summary: A threat actor has been targeting organizations across multiple sectors with voice-based fake security requests that ask Microsoft 365 users to enroll a new Entra passkey. [...]

articles.title: Accenture Confirms Data Breach After Hacker Claims Source Code Theft
articles.summary: The professional services giant says it contained the incident, remediated its source, and experienced no operational or service delivery impact. The post Accenture Confirms Data Breach After Hacker Claims Source Code Theft appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: China-Linked APT Expands Arsenal With New ‘Leash’ Backdoors
articles.summary: Cisco says the threat actor behind the LapDogs campaign has expanded its SOHO router malware toolkit with LongLeash, DogLeash, and JarLeash backdoors. The post China-Linked APT Expands Arsenal With New ‘Leash’ Backdoors appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: New HalluSquatting Attack Could Trick AI Coding Assistants Into Installing Botnet Malware
articles.summary: AI coding assistants have a habit of making things up. Ask one to fetch a popular tool, and it will sometimes hand back a real-sounding name for a project that does not exist. articles.summary: Ubiquiti has shipped updates to address multiple critical security flaws impacting UniFi Connect, UniFi Talk, UniFi Access, UniFi Protect, and UniFi OS that could result in privilege escalation and arbitrary command execution. articles.title: 3 Ways AI Powers Service Desk Attacks and How to Prevent Them
articles.summary: Specops Software explains how AI is making service desk impersonation attacks more convincing, personalized, and scalable, along with practical steps organizations can take to strengthen onboarding and identity verification. [...]

articles.title: Spain arrests alleged supporter of pro-Russian hacktivist groups after FBI tip
articles.summary: An FBI tip linked a man living in Spain to the hacking groups CyberArmy of Russia Reborn (CARR), Z-Pentest and NoName057(16).

articles.summary: A recent EvilTokens campaign targeting businesses across the US and Europe is exposing a new email security blind spot. articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has ordered government agencies to patch an actively exploited maximum-severity flaw in the Adobe ColdFusion commercial web app development platform by Friday. [...]

articles.summary: Researchers at Nebula Security have disclosed GhostLock (CVE-2026-43499), a 15-year-old Linux kernel flaw that lets any logged-in user take full root control of a machine that has not been patched. The vulnerable code has shipped by default in essentially every mainstream distrib

articles.summary: Chinese hackers tracked as 'UAT-7810' are actively evolving their malware to expand their Operational Relay Box (ORB) network by compromising internet-facing networking devices, primarily unpatched Ruckus routers. [...]

articles.summary: A hidden authentication backdoor has been found in multiple Tenda router firmware versions, potentially allowing an attacker to gain administrative access to the device's web management panel. [...]

articles.summary: A new Android malware operation called RedWing is being rented out on Telegram as a ready-made bank-fraud service. articles.summary: A critical flaw in Google's Dialogflow CX could have let an attacker with edit rights on one Code Block-enabled agent compromise other Code Block-enabled agents in the same Google Cloud project. From there, they could read live conversations, steal the data users shared, and make the bots send attac

articles.title: Britain plans to build autonomous AI 'Cyber Shield' to defend nation
articles.summary: The capability, called Cyber Shield, is designed to counter a threat the National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) said could see attackers “move at machine speed and greater scale, reducing opportunities for detection and response.”

articles.title: DEBULL Tooling Abuses Microsoft Device-Code Flow to Target M365 Accounts
articles.summary: A Microsoft 365 device code phishing campaign has been observed leveraging collaboration-themed lures to take control of victim accounts between the last week of June 2026 and into early July, per findings from ZeroBEC. articles.title: Major Japanese telco says cyberattack exposed 12 million emails
articles.summary: The company said the breach affected an email system used to manage customer email accounts, webmail services and email storage for five Japanese internet service providers.

articles.title: Public GitHub Issue Could Trick GitHub Agentic Workflows Into Leaking Private Repo Data
articles.summary: A public issue can trick GitHub Agentic Workflows into leaking the contents of an organization's private repositories, researchers at Noma Security have shown. The attacker needs only to open a normal-looking issue on a public repository, with no stolen credentials and no access to the organization.

articles.title: The GitHub Actions Attack Pattern Your CI Security Scanners Miss

articles.summary: U.S. prosecutors linked an alleged Scattered Spider hacker to a break-in at a luxury jewelry retailer using a persistent Windows device ID, according to a newly unsealed federal complaint. articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched critical session isolation vulnerability in Writer, an enterprise generative artificial intelligence (AI) platform, that could result in cross-tenant compromise. articles.title: UK cyber pledge draws only a handful of top firms despite ministerial appeal
articles.summary: Those that did sign include large firms such as Aviva, the London Stock Exchange Group and Marks & Spencer, which lost hundreds of millions of pounds in a cyberattack last year, as well as small cybersecurity consultancies.
